messages.content: Write a forum convo in May 2011 titled "The Death of the CW4Kids" where due to 4Kids's financial troubles, the block will be handed back, and its name is reverting next week, and the website already has, The lineup is staying the same for now (so you'll still get Yu Gi Oh, GoGoRiki, etc)

**Thread Title:** The Death of the CW4Kids - End of an Era?

**UltraNostalgicFan:** Hey everyone, just read some news that's a bit of a shocker but not entirely unexpected - due to financial troubles, 4Kids is handing back its Saturday morning block, and it seems like "The CW4Kids" as we know it is coming to an end. As early as next week, they're reverting back to its original name, and even the website has already undergone changes. At least for the time being, the lineup reportedly won't change much. Yes, that means Yu-Gi-Oh!, GoGoRiki, and the rest are staying put for now. Thoughts?

**SaturdayAMFan:** Wow, that's quite a blow to my childhood nostalgia! 4Kids had been an integral part of our weekend routines. Although I'm relieved that shows like Yu-Gi-Oh! are staying, the whole scenario feels like the end of an era. Any idea what led to their financial troubles?

**AnimeLoverX:** @SaturdayAMFan, from what I've gathered, 4Kids has been struggling financially for quite some time mainly due to a combination of bad investments and legal battles that drained their resources. It's a real shame because they brought so much anime to western audiences, even though their localizations were... contentious at times.

**CartoonConnoisseur:** This is bittersweet news indeed. While it's sad to see The CW4Kids go, perhaps a new direction could breathe fresh life into Saturday mornings. Still, it's an end of an era. 4Kids was a big part of why anime and other international cartoons became accessible and popular here. Their legacy, for better or worse, will be remembered.

**LegalEagle101:** There's also the matter of what happens next. While the lineup remains the same for now, it's only a matter of time before things start to change. I'm curious to see if the new management will introduce different shows or stick with the current formula. Either way, it's an uncertain future for what was once a staple of weekend television.

**NostalgicCollector:** This is sad news, but let's not forget all the great times 4Kids gave us. From waking up early to catch the latest episodes of our favorite shows to discussing them with friends at school, it was all part of growing up. I hope the new management keeps the spirit alive, but it won't be the same. Saturday mornings have officially changed.

**MangaManiac:** While I have mixed feelings about some of 4Kids' localizations, there's no denying the impact they had. It's a bit concerning, though, that the financial issues got to this point. Looking ahead, I just hope that the shows stay accessible and that the transition is smooth enough that the audience doesn't suffer.

**UltraNostalgicFan:** It's clear we all have our memories and opinions about 4Kids and its influence. While change is inevitable, it's crucial to remember the joy and excitement brought to our weekends. Let's keep an eye on how things evolve and support our favorite shows through these transitions. Here's to hoping for a bright future for Saturday morning cartoons, whatever form they may take!

**Thread Locked**

**ModeratorNote:** This thread has been locked due to exceeding maximum comment capacity. Please feel free to continue the discussion in a new thread. Thanks for sharing your thoughts and memories about The CW4Kids and 4Kids Entertainment!
Write a tweet where the website is already redirecting from cw4kids.com

🚨Big changes ahead!🚨 The #CW4Kids website is already redirecting! End of an era but excited to see what the future holds. 📺✨ Shows like #YuGiOh and #GoGoRiki are sticking around for now. Let's hope the transition is smooth! What are your thoughts? #SaturdayMornings #EndOfAnEra 🍿📚
Write an ad saying that CW4Kids becomes Kids WB next week

🎉 Exciting News Alert! 🎉

Starting next week, say hello to nostalgia and welcome back an icon - *CW4Kids evolves into Kids' WB*! 🚀🌈

Prepare yourself for a whirlwind of your favorite shows and beloved classics as we embark on a thrilling journey back to where it all began. Yes, that's right – the legendary #KidsWB is making a grand return, bringing along a blend of adventure, laughter, and memories!

📅 **Mark Your Calendars!** Dive into the fun starting next Saturday morning. Whether you're a fan of the high-stakes action in #YuGiOh, the hilarious escapades in #GoGoRiki, or looking forward to discovering new adventures, there's something special for everyone. 🎬🍿

🔗 And guess what? A sneak peek is just a click away. Head over to our website (now redirecting from cw4kids.com) to get a glimpse of what's coming. You won't want to miss a single moment!

🎈 **What's in Store?**
- Your favorite CW4Kids shows are here to stay! 
- Exciting new additions and spectacular surprises!
- A unique blend of entertainment that captures the spirit of the classic Kids' WB!

👧👦 So, gather your friends and family for a blast from the past that's also a glimpse into the future - Kids' WB is here to reignite your Saturday mornings and fill them with joy, excitement, and that unmistakable touch of magic. Let the countdown begin!

🌟 **Stay Tuned!** Keep your eyes peeled on our social media for updates, teasers, and more exciting news. Let's make Saturday mornings awesome again!

#KidsWB #SaturdayMorningFun #Nostalgia #NewBeginnings #FamilyEntertainment

Write a forum convo where it looks like 4Kids are just changing the name back early to ease us back in (this is why the digital video presentation style and "Sign On" slogan survive just with the KWB logo) so we wont know till September what the real Kids WB 2.0 is

**Thread Title:** Kids' WB 2.0 - A Gradual Transition or a Surprise in the Making?

**ToonFan88:** Did anyone else catch the news that 4Kids is transitioning back to Kids' WB next week? Seems like they're easing us in with the name change first. The "Sign On" slogan and digital video presentation style are sticking around, just branded with the KWB logo now. Thoughts on this slow roll-out?

**NostalgiaNerd:** Sounds like they’re taking a gentle approach to the transition, maybe to keep the current audience hooked while they prep for the bigger revamp in September. I'm curious about what "Kids' WB 2.0" will bring to the table. Will it be a blend of the old and new? Either way, keeping some elements like the digital presentation style feels like a nod to continuity.

**SaturdayMornSkeptic:** I’m cautiously optimistic but also a bit skeptical. The name drop to Kids’ WB certainly has my nostalgia piqued, but I wonder if the content will match the hype. Changing visuals without touching the substance doesn’t mean much, but I guess we’ll have to wait until September for the real verdict.

**DigitalToonie:** Honestly, I think using the "Sign On" slogan and keeping the digital presentation is a smart move. It maintains a bit of the old 4Kids vibe while promising something new. If they can strike the right balance between the legacy of Kids' WB and the expectations of today’s audience, we might be in for a treat.

**RetroRewinder:** I'm all in for a surprise, but the anticipation is killing me! Kids' WB was a huge part of my childhood Saturdays. I hope they reintroduce some classic shows alongside new content. September can’t come soon enough, but till then, I'm glad they're not making drastic changes out of the gate.

**ToonTechie:** Let's not forget the potential digital enhancements and innovations they might bring to the table. Keeping the digital video presentation style could suggest they plan to integrate more modern or interactive elements into the broadcast. Could be a game-changer if done right.

**PanelPicker:** The slow transition is clever marketing, keeping us engaged and building anticipation. Changing the name back to Kids' WB without immediately overhauling everything allows the audience to adjust. I'm intrigued by what the final version will look like. Will Kids' WB 2.0 manage to recapture the magic and blend it with contemporary appeals?

**ToonFan88:** Great points, everyone. It seems we're all a mix of hopeful, curious, and a tad skeptical. It’ll be interesting to see how the transition unfolds and what surprises Kids' WB has in store for us this September. Until then, let's enjoy the nostalgia wave and keep speculating on what's to come! 

**Thread Locked by Moderator**

**ModNote:** Thanks for participating in this vibrant discussion! Please keep an eye out for future threads as we get closer to the September unveiling of Kids' WB 2.0. Remember to stay respectful and share your excitement and concerns alike. Happy viewing, everyone!

Write a forum convo with the current lineup (almost all 4Kids shows, with the only non 4Kids show being Will and Dewitt) Things are a tiny bit different with the Super Summer Lineup, which adds Regular Show, Johnny Test and Tom and Jerry Tales, The promo VO is still a 4kids one though,

**Thread Title:** Kid's WB Super Summer Lineup - A Fresh Mix or More of the Same?

**CartoonCraze101:** Hey fellow animation aficionados! Have you guys checked out the new Super Summer Lineup for Kids’ WB? It’s almost all the classic 4Kids shows we've grown to love (or tolerate) with the intriguing addition of *Regular Show, Johnny Test*, and *Tom and Jerry Tales*. And yep, the promos still have that unmistakable 4Kids voiceover charm. Thoughts on the blend?

**NostalgicToonFan:** Really excited to see *Regular Show* making it into the lineup. It brings a different vibe compared to the typical 4Kids fare, and it's a fresh breath of fun. *Johnny Test* and *Tom and Jerry Tales* are solid choices too, though *Tom and Jerry Tales* feels more in line with the traditional Saturday morning cartoon feel.

**ModernToonWatcher:** It's an interesting mix for sure. The inclusion of non-4Kids shows like *Will and DeWitt* alongside *Regular Show* and the others shows they're attempting to diversify the lineup. I'm curious how they'll all mesh with the classics under the Kids' WB branding. Still, the 4Kids-style promos are a bit of a throwback - not sure if that’s a good or bad thing yet.

**SaturdayMorningPurist:** Honestly, I'm just glad to see Kids' WB holding onto some of its identity with shows like *Yu-Gi-Oh!* still on the roster. The new additions are fine, but I hope they don’t overshadow the essence of what made Saturday mornings great. *Regular Show* is an excellent pickup, though. Love that show!

**AnimationAnalyst:** The strategic mix of shows in the Super Summer Lineup feels like a bid to capture both nostalgic audiences and newer viewers. *Regular Show* has a cross-generational appeal that complements the more traditional vibe of *Johnny Test* and *Tom and Jerry Tales*. However, the cohesion of the lineup will really depend on scheduling and promo integration. Still unsure about the 4Kids voiceover style; it might not resonate with everyone in 2021.

**ToonTechie:** Does anyone else think the 4Kids-type promo VO adds a layer of nostalgia that might actually draw in viewers? It’s distinctive and immediately recognizable to anyone who grew up with 4Kids programming. Sure, it’s a bit campy, but it ties the old and new elements of the lineup together in a unique way.

**WeekendWatcher:** Caught the promo for the Super Summer Lineup and it piqued my interest. *Regular Show* is a gem that could seriously revitalize Saturday mornings. As for *Tom and Jerry Tales*, you can't go wrong with classic chase shenanigans. I’m keen to see how *Johnny Test* fits into the mix since it’s hit or miss for some folks. The promo voice-over threw me back to my cereal-in-front-of-the-TV days.

**CartoonCraze101:** Great insights, everyone! It seems like there’s cautious optimism about the new additions and how they’ll weave into the fabric of what Kids’ WB is transforming into. And maybe, just maybe, the retro 4Kids promo vibe is the cherry on top that will bridge generations of viewers. Only time will tell if this Super Summer Lineup will make a splash or just ripple through our Saturday mornings.

**Thread Locked by Moderator**

**ModNote:** Thanks for engaging in this lively discussion about the Super Summer Lineup on Kids’ WB! It’s wonderful to see such passionate viewpoints. As summer progresses, let’s reconvene and share our thoughts on how well the new additions are faring. Until then, keep the nostalgia alive and your remotes ready!
Write the new schedule, which lasts from 7am to 12pm

### Kids' WB Super Summer Lineup Schedule

**Let the Summer Fun Begin on Kids' WB!**

Dive into the ultimate mix of action, comedy, and classic toon adventures every Saturday from 7 am to 12 pm. Check out the brand-new schedule designed to kick off your weekends in style!

---

**7:00 am** - **Tom and Jerry Tales**  
Kickstart your morning with timeless chases and hilarious antics in Tom and Jerry Tales! The legendary cat and mouse duo is back in new adventures that will leave you laughing out loud.

**7:30 am** - **Will and Dewitt**  
Join Will and his talking frog Dewitt on their fun-filled journeys! It’s all about friendship, solving problems, and the exciting world of imagination.

**8:00 am** - **Yu-Gi-Oh!**  
Dive into the world of Duel Monsters with *Yu-Gi-Oh!* Watch Yugi and his friends battle it out in epic card duels to become the ultimate Duel Masters.

**8:30 am** - **Sonic X**  
Speed into adventure with *Sonic X!* Follow Sonic, Tails, Knuckles, and the rest of the gang as they fight to protect their friends and their home from the evil Dr. Eggman.

**9:00 am** - **Regular Show**  
Get ready for surreal misadventures with Mordecai and Rigby in *Regular Show.* It's anything but regular when these two friends find themselves in absurd situations.

**9:30 am** - **Johnny Test**  
Wacky experiments, supercharged adventures, and a fearless boy with flame-haired sisters! Witness the chaotic life of Johnny Test and the crazy world around him.

**10:00 am** - **Pokemon**  
Embark on thrilling adventures in the world of Pokémon with Ash Ketchum and his trusty Pikachu. Experience the excitement of Pokémon battles, the joy of making new friends, and the dream of becoming a Pokémon Master.

**10:30 am** - **Magi-Nation**  
Follow the journey of Tony and his friends as they explore the magical world of the Moonlands. Together, they must thwart evil forces and discover secret mysteries within the *Magi-Nation*.

**11:00 am** - **Dragon Ball Z Kai**  
Experience the thrill of *Dragon Ball Z Kai*, where Goku and his friends protect Earth from villains. Get pumped for action-packed battles and epic Saiyan transformations.

**11:30 am** - **The Spectacular Spider-Man**  
End your morning with *The Spectacular Spider-Man*! Swing into action with Peter Parker as he battles the baddies of New York City while juggling the challenges of being a teenager.

---

### Note to Viewers:

This Super Summer Lineup is designed to bring you the best of both worlds - nostalgia from your beloved classics and excitement from new adventures! So, pour yourself a big bowl of Saturday morning cereal, and let's make this summer spectacular on Kids' WB. Keep an eye out for special episode marathons and surprise guest appearances!

Enjoy the shows, and don't forget to share your favorite moments with us on social media using the hashtag #KidsWBSummerFun!

**Sign On to Kids' WB - Where every Saturday is a new adventure!**

Write a follow up in September where the change is moving along great, Right now, Cartoon Network announcers are starting to replace 4Kids TV ones, and we see the full new Kids WB lineup, starting from the first week in September, Also, the new look is the iconic water tower bumpers and rubbery logo mascot, the Fall promo has this

### **Kids' WB Fall Season Kickoff - A New Era Begins!**

**Exciting News, Animation Enthusiasts!**

As September rolls in, we're thrilled to announce that the transformation of Kids' WB is moving along spectacularly! The evolution has been embraced by fans old and new, marking the beginning of an exciting new era for Saturday morning cartoons.

**What’s New?**

- **Fresh Voices:** You might have noticed a change in the airwaves lately. Our promos and announcements have welcomed the dynamic voices from Cartoon Network's talented announcer team! They’re bringing a fresh, energetic vibe to our lineup, perfectly complementing our new look and feel.

- **Iconic Imagery Returns:** Nostalgia lovers, rejoice! The iconic water tower bumpers are back, introducing shows and sending you off to commercials with that classic WB flair. And keep an eye out for our new rubbery logo mascot, bouncing around and getting into all sorts of fun mischief in our fall promo!

- **The Fall Lineup:** It’s the moment you've been waiting for – our **full new Kids' WB lineup** for the first week of September! We’ve kept your favorites and added some new adventures to the mix. Ready to dive in?

---

### **Saturday Morning Schedule from 7 am to 12 pm:**

**7:00 am** - **The Looney Tunes Show**  
Wake up to the hilarious antics of Bugs Bunny, Daffy Duck, and the rest of the Looney Tunes gang as they navigate the modern world.

**7:30 am** - **Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated**  
Join Scooby and the Mystery Inc. gang as they uncover the spooky mysteries of Crystal Cove, the most haunted place on Earth.

**8:00 am** - **Teen Titans Go!**  
Gear up for superheroic comedy with the Teen Titans! Superpowers and super misadventures await in this fan-favorite show.

**8:30 am** - **Pokémon: The Series**  
Ash Ketchum’s journey continues with new friends, new Pokémon, and epic battles. It’s a whole new Pokémon world to explore!

**9:00 am** - **Adventure Time**  
Adventure awaits in the Land of Ooo! Join Finn the Human and Jake the Dog on their quests for fun and righteousness.

**9:30 am** - **Regular Show**  
Back by popular demand! Mordecai and Rigby’s everyday job leads to extraordinary misadventures. Can they turn things around before it’s too late?

**10:00 am** - **Justice League Action**  
The world's greatest superheroes unite to fight threats too big for any one hero to handle. High stakes and high action with the Justice League!

**10:30 am** - **Tom and Jerry Tales**  
Classic chase comedy with a modern twist. Tom and Jerry are at it again, and no “truce” is in sight.

**11:00 am** - **Batman: The Brave and the Bold**  
Batman teams up with heroes from across the DC Universe, embarking on new adventures and facing epic challenges.

**11:30 am** - **The Spectacular Spider-Man**  
Peter Parker swings back into action. Between the classroom and fighting crime as Spider-Man, his life’s anything but boring.

---

**Mark Your Calendars!** The fun starts the first week of September. Kids' WB is your destination for laughter, adventure, and the best in animation. With our updated look and lineup, Saturday mornings will never be the same!

Share your excitement with us using the hashtag **#KidsWBNewEra** and stay tuned for more updates, surprises, and animated fun on Kids' WB – **Where every weekend is an adventure!**

**Sign On to the New Kids' WB - It's Time to Animate Your World!**

Write a Wikipedia page (in this universe) titled "Kids' WB (revival), the History page says it came into existence due to 4Kids handing back the CW4Kids due to financial troubles, and them slowly giving the block back to Warner, As such, Sonic X, GoGoRiki, etc were initially part of the lineup, Also have a Current and Former programming lineup, with Xiaolin Showdown in Upcoming programming (replacing SSM)

# Kids' WB (Revival)

Kids' WB, a block of children's programming that originally aired on The WB Television Network and later on The CW, saw its revival in the early 2020s after a period of absence. The revival was spurred by 4Kids Entertainment handing back the CW4Kids block to Warner Bros. due to financial difficulties, marking a significant transition in the landscape of Saturday morning children's programming.

## History

The original Kids' WB block was launched on September 9, 1995, and continued to broadcast on The WB and subsequently The CW until May 17, 2008. During its initial run, Kids' WB was known for showcasing a mix of original and acquired programming, including iconic shows such as "Pokémon," "Yu-Gi-Oh!," "Batman: The Animated Series," and "Animaniacs."

The revived Kids' WB came into existence as 4Kids Entertainment faced financial hardships, leading to the decision to hand back the CW4Kids block to Warner Bros. This transition began a process of slowly reintegrating Warner Bros.' portfolio of animated content back into the lineup, while initially retaining shows like "Sonic X" and "GoGoRiki" that were part of the previous iteration.

### Transition Period

The transition to the new Kids' WB was strategic and gradual. Initially, the lineup remained similar to that of the CW4Kids' final offerings, blending 4Kids' acquisitions with Warner Bros.' iconic franchises. This approach allowed the block to maintain its existing audience while preparing for a more distinct shift towards Warner-owned content.

Promotional efforts emphasized the return of the beloved Kids' WB brand, leveraging nostalgia and the legacy of Warner Bros.' animation to attract viewers. The new Kids' WB also reintroduced classic branding elements, such as the iconic water tower and whimsical bumpers, modernizing them for a contemporary audience.

## Current Programming

- "The Looney Tunes Show"
- "Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated"
- "Teen Titans Go!"
- "Pokémon: The Series"
- "Adventure Time"
- "Regular Show"
- "Justice League Action"
- "Tom and Jerry Tales"
- "Batman: The Brave and the Bold"
- "The Spectacular Spider-Man"

## Former Programming

- "Sonic X"
- "GoGoRiki"
- "Yu-Gi-Oh!"
- "Magi-Nation"
- "Dragon Ball Z Kai"

## Upcoming Programming

- "Xiaolin Showdown" (Replacing "The Spectacular Spider-Man")

### Revival Impact

The revival of Kids' WB has been received positively by audiences, blending the nostalgia of classic Warner Bros. animations with the addition of modern, acclaimed programming. The return has been viewed as a revitalization of the Saturday morning cartoon tradition, offering a blend of adventure, humor, and superhero-themed content for a new generation of viewers while paying homage to its roots.

Through strategic programming and a homage to its legacy, Kids' WB's revival serves as a testament to the enduring appeal of quality children's programming and the iconic status of Warner Bros.' animated characters and stories.
Write a forum convo where Spidey is being snapped away in mid October, and quote the Infinity War scene, and incidentally XS promos are already running

**Thread Title:** Spidey Getting Snapped Away in Mid-October - Infinity War Vibes?

**WebSlingerFan99:** Guys, just saw the announcement that *The Spectacular Spider-Man* is leaving the Kids’ WB lineup in mid-October. Can’t help but feel like it’s Peter Parker saying, “Mr. Stark, I don’t feel so good...” 😢 Anyone else got those Infinity War vibes?

**NostalgiaNerd456:** @WebSlingerFan99 Totally feeling that. It’s like watching Spider-Man get snapped away all over again. Wasn’t ready to say goodbye to this show. It’s spectacularly unfortunate. 😞 And with XS promos already running, feels like they’re moving fast on this.

**ToonTacticTom:** Yeah, it’s sad to see Spidey go. That whole Infinity War reference hits too close to home. But have you guys seen the XS promos? Looks like Kids’ WB is getting ready to introduce us to something new and exciting.

**PixelParker:** Can't believe Spidey is swinging out of the lineup. “I don’t want to go…” – pretty much sums up my feelings. But I’m intrigued by *Xiaolin Showdown* coming back. Nostalgia hitting hard with that one!

**HelmetHairHector:** Honestly, this lineup change feels so sudden. I get that shows come and go, but Spidey was a staple. “This is no place to die” – except it feels like a small part of my Saturday mornings is dying. Curious about XS, though; those promos hint at some solid action.

**ComicBookCarl:** It’s a tough pill to swallow. Watching *The Spectacular Spider-Man* get dusted is like a piece of my childhood fading away. Yet, in the ashes of one hero's departure, another rises. *Xiaolin Showdown* was a favorite of mine, so I'm cautiously optimistic.

**SaturdayScribe:** Transition periods are always a mix of excitement and melancholy. Spidey’s exit, coupled with the Infinity War feels, makes it bittersweet. Yet, the essence of Saturday mornings is change and anticipation for new adventures. Let’s hold onto hope for what *Xiaolin Showdown* will bring.

**WebSlingerFan99:** Thanks, everyone, for sharing your thoughts and feelings. It seems we’re all in this mix of mourning and looking forward to what’s next. Here’s to hoping that *Xiaolin Showdown* fills the webbed shoes left behind by Spidey and brings its own brand of action and storytelling to our weekends. 🕷🌀

**Thread Locked by Moderator**

**ModNote:** Thanks for participating in this heartfelt discussion. It’s evident *The Spectacular Spider-Man* has left its mark on many of us. As we bid farewell to our beloved hero, let’s also welcome *Xiaolin Showdown* with open arms and an open heart. Stay tuned for more updates and keep the spirit of Saturday mornings alive!
